blocks.text: The word which the prophet Jeremiah spoke to Baruch son of Neriah, when he was writing these words in a scroll at Jeremiah’s dictation, in the fourth year of King Jehoiakim son of Josiah of Judah:
Thus said the Lord, the God of Israel, concerning you, Baruch:
You say, “Woe is me! The Lord has added grief to my pain. I am worn out with groaning, and I have found no rest.”
Thus shall you speak to him: “Thus said the Lord: I am going to overthrow what I have built, and uproot what I have planted—this applies to the whole land.

And do you expect great things for yourself? Don’t expect them. For I am going to bring disaster upon all flesh—declares the Lord—but I will at least grant you your life in all the places where you may go.”
